message:
feat: add cooperative yielding budgets to stream consumers to prevent executor monopolization

Add per-poll cooperative budgets to seven stream consumer futures/streams
that previously contained unbounded drain loops. When an always-ready upstream
stream is paired with these combinators, the inner loop could run to
exhaustion in a single poll call, starving all sibling tasks on the same
executor. Each combinator now counts items processed per poll and yields
Poll::Pending (with a self-wake via wake_by_ref) after hitting the budget.

Affected combinators and their budget constants:
- Any / All (any_all.rs): ANY_ALL_COOPERATIVE_BUDGET = 1024
- Collect (collect.rs): COLLECT_COOPERATIVE_BUDGET = 1024
- Count (count.rs): COUNT_COOPERATIVE_BUDGET = 1024
- Filter / FilterMap (filter.rs): FILTER_REJECTION_BUDGET = 1024
- Fold (fold.rs): FOLD_COOPERATIVE_BUDGET = 1024
- ForEach / ForEachAsync (for_each.rs): FOR_EACH_COOPERATIVE_BUDGET = 1024
- Skip (skip.rs): SKIP_COOPERATIVE_BUDGET = 1024

Each combinator now includes a test with an AlwaysReadyCounter stream that
validates: (a) the first poll returns Pending after exactly budget items,
(b) partial state is preserved across the yield, (c) a self-wake is
registered, and (d) the second poll completes normally.

This is the same pattern already established by the Throttle combinator's
MAX_SUPPRESSED_DRAIN_PER_POLL in a prior commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
